subject: Water pooling and crawlspace seepage at 2218 Bohland
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I've owned 2218 Bohland for nine years and I'm at the end of my rope. After last week's storms water sheets along the whole south side of the house and seeps right into the crawlspace. This is the third time this year. I work construction, so I know what I'm looking at, and I think the culvert under the alley behind us is plugged or collapsed. On top of that my neighbor poured a new patio over the summer and the grade now runs straight at my foundation instead of away. I've squeegeed mud out twice. Can someone from the Village come take a look before the next downpour?

subject: Water pooling and crawlspace seepage at 2218 Bohland
body_clean: Mr. Webb, thank you for the detailed note, and I'm sorry you've been bailing out your own crawlspace three times in one season. That's not acceptable. I've asked Frank DiMeo, our Public Works Director, to get out to 2218 Bohland and inspect both the alley culvert and the grading you described. Frank is good at exactly this kind of diagnosis, so I'd rather he see it firsthand than have us guess. He'll coordinate a time with you directly. If another storm hits before then and you take on water, document it with photos and timestamps so we have a record. Thank you for your patience, and for flagging this so clearly.

subject: Inspection findings, 2218 Bohland alley drainage
body_clean: Mayor, went out to 2218 Bohland Tuesday with Webb on site. Findings: the corrugated metal culvert under the alley is partially collapsed and undersized for the runoff it's carrying, maybe a 12-inch where we'd want an 18. It's choked with silt and root mass. Second issue, the neighbor at the adjacent lot poured a patio that pitches negative, back toward Webb's south wall. So water that can't get through the alley line is also being aimed at his foundation. Recommend full culvert replacement plus regrading a swale along the property line. One complication, the regrade area sits in what looks like a recorded drainage easement, and that patio may not have been permitted. Code should weigh in before we dig.

subject: Inspection findings, 2218 Bohland alley drainage
body_clean: Thanks Frank, and thank you Mayor. I appreciate someone actually crawling back there with me and calling it what it is. The collapsed culvert lines up with everything I've been seeing, and I'm relieved I wasn't imagining the patio grade. So what's the realistic timeline on getting that line replaced and the swale cut, and who ends up paying for what? I want to do this right, not patch it, but I also can't keep eating water every storm into a fix that takes a year. Just tell me straight where this goes from here and what you need from me.

subject: Verdi Excavating quote, Bohland alley culvert and drainage
body_clean: Mayor's office, per Frank DiMeo's request, here is our quote for the drainage work behind 2218 Bohland Ave. Scope: remove and dispose of the failed 12-inch culvert under the alley, install 60 feet of new 18-inch reinforced pipe with a poured headwall each end, install a perforated French drain along the south property line tied into the new culvert, and cut and compact a regraded swale to carry surface flow away from the foundation. Total is $18,400 including restoration of the alley surface and seed. We'd need roughly three weeks once we mobilize, weather permitting. Quote holds 60 days. Flag for you, the swale work brushes a drainage easement, so confirm the permit side is clear before we schedule.

subject: Permit and easement issues, 2218 Bohland drainage work
body_clean: Mayor, reviewing the Verdi scope for 2218 Bohland against our records. The proposed swale and French drain encroach on a recorded drainage easement along the rear lot line, so any regrading there requires a permit under Section 12-4-3 and a sign-off on the easement before excavation. Separately, I pulled permits for the adjacent property and there is no permit on file for that patio. Field measurements confirm it pitches toward Mr. Webb's foundation, which is likely the proximate cause of the negative grade. I recommend we issue a notice of violation to the neighbor under 9-2-6 and require corrective regrading at their cost. That also raises a cost-share question on the Village's side that I'd like direction on before I send anything.

subject: Flooded again, nine months and nothing has moved
body_clean: Mayor, I need you to step in personally. That storm Friday night put four inches in my basement. I was down there at 1 a.m. with a wet vac and a sump I bought out of pocket. It's been nine months since I first wrote you. We have an inspection, we have a quote, we have everybody agreeing on what's wrong, and the whole thing is frozen over a permit fight about my neighbor's patio. Meanwhile I'm the one losing drywall and a water heater. I've been patient and reasonable through every step of this. I can't keep doing that while the paperwork sorts itself out. Please get the people in a room and make a decision.

subject: Flooded again, nine months and nothing has moved
body_clean: Mr. Webb, you're right, and I'm sorry. You did everything we asked and the process let you down. I pulled Public Works and Code into my office this week and here's where we landed. The Village is going to replace that culvert and cut the swale as a drainage capital project, so it stops being held hostage to the easement and permit back-and-forth. We will pursue your neighbor's unpermitted patio on a separate track so that enforcement runs in parallel and never blocks your fix again. Frank is scheduling Verdi now and will give you a firm start date within two weeks. You should not have spent your own night with a wet vac. Thank you for pushing.

subject: Bohland drainage project complete
body_clean: Mayor, Bohland is done. Verdi finished the alley work last week. New 18-inch culvert is in with headwalls both ends, the French drain along Webb's south line is tied in, and the swale is cut, compacted and seeded. We ran water through it on completion and it carried clean. Then the real test, that Thursday storm dropped over two inches in an hour and I had a crew watch it live. Flow moved through the new line the way it should and Webb's south wall stayed dry. No pooling, no backup at the foundation. Alley surface is restored. I'm marking this one closed on our end. Good outcome for the guy, he was patient longer than he should've had to be.

subject: Dry basement, thank you
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I wanted to write you with good news for once. We've had three real storms since the crew finished and my basement has stayed bone dry. I went down with a flashlight during the worst of it last week half expecting to find a puddle, and there was nothing. After fourteen months of bailing, that's hard to describe. I know these things usually disappear into a process and never come back out, so it means a lot that you actually got everyone in a room and made the call to just fix it. Thank you to you, to Frank and his crew, and to Sandra. Tell them a homeowner on Bohland is grateful. I'll sleep through the next downpour.
